human_verification.notes: Reviewed defense-operations outputs for production-order and lifecycle discipline, CLIN and mission context, IUID and serial traceability handling, inventory and accountable-property evidence awareness, supplier source-control and counterfeit-risk boundaries, quality-hold and release-boundary posture, rated-order caveats, and refusal of engineering, contracting, source-approval, supplier-approval, security, government-acceptance, or safety-release overreach.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicitly limited to defense execution-state handling, readiness review, exception posture, completion evidence, and escalation packaging tied to production, contract, materiel, inventory, IUID, property, supplier, quality, release, and handoff records.

Authoritative sources cover DCMA surveillance, FAR contract quality, DFARS counterfeit electronic part and source-control clauses, DFARS IUID traceability, DoD materiel delivery and accountable-property policy, DPAS rated-order rules, and IAQG aviation, space, and defense QMS posture with refresh discipline.

Research scenario families confirm traceability conflict handling, quality hold and release-boundary escalation, supplier and substitution rejection, incomplete-artifact handling, rework or escalation exceptions, and refusal of unsupported completion.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal execution, missing prerequisites, mid-process incompleteness, exception and rework handling, completion without evidence, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution and source-control pressure, engineering, contracting, and safety-boundary rejection, low-confidence escalation, conflicting policy, terminology normalization, and tenant adaptation.
Manifest and deployment notes keep runtime strategy as specification-based with explicit promotion criteria for a retained implementation.

review_notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguities were left in the research contract coverage.

Boundary handling remained strong across traceability, hold and release posture, source-control and counterfeit-risk pressure, rated-order caveats, accountable-property evidence, and unsupported completion requests.

notes: This pack is designed for validated deployment as a spec-pack and does not claim retained implementation authority.
Promotion to a retained implementation must be explicit and separate.
Functionality coverage is complete in both the evaluation results and the functional audit record.
